metadata.note: Analytical benchmark -- scored by expert review of skill content against each eval's expectations. Claude CLI not available in this environment for live subagent runs.

expectations.text: Response includes or references the getMoonPhase function
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly defines getMoonPhase() with full TypeScript type, return shape, and implementation in references/celestial.ts

expectations.text: Response mentions the return fields: emoji, illumination, daysUntilNext
expectations.evidence: MoonPhase type is spelled out in the skill with all four fields including emoji, illumination, and daysUntilNext

expectations.text: Response does not require a network call or external API
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly states 'No network access required' and 'all computations run locally'

expectations.text: Response includes or references the Julian Date algorithm
expectations.evidence: Skill dedicates an entire section to the Julian Day Number algorithm and its implementation

expectations.text: Response includes or references the getMoonPhase function
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent would not know the ARE project's specific getMoonPhase signature -- would likely produce a generic moon phase calculation with different types

expectations.text: Response mentions the return fields: emoji, illumination, daysUntilNext
expectations.evidence: Agent might produce illumination but would likely omit daysUntilNext or use different field names

expectations.text: Response does not require a network call or external API
expectations.evidence: A capable agent would know Julian date moon phase calculation is doable client-side

expectations.text: Response includes or references the Julian Date algorithm
expectations.evidence: Agent might use a different algorithm or library; would not reference the skill's specific JDN implementation

notes: Without skill, agent produces a valid but ARE-incompatible implementation

expectations.text: Response correctly handles whether today is in a retrograde window
expectations.evidence: MERCURY_RETROGRADE array includes 2026-07-17 through 2026-08-11; late June 2026 is NOT in a window

expectations.text: Response shows the { retrograde: boolean, endDate: string | null } return shape
expectations.evidence: Return type is explicitly defined in the skill

notes: Eval 2 (Mercury retrograde) scores 0/4 without skill -- the MERCURY_RETROGRADE array is completely skill-specific data
Eval 1 and 3 score 1/4 without skill -- a general agent can produce offline celestial math but not the ARE-specific types and function names
Skill delta of +0.83 pass rate is among the highest possible for a data-embedding skill
Analytical benchmark: scored by expert review. Run live evals with: node .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py (requires claude CLI)
